title: migration v7 comment says 'partial index' but index does not use WHERE clause value in key
description: db.rs migration v7 creates: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS notes_unspent ON notes(spent) WHERE spent = 0. The comment says 'Partial index on spent = 0 only — SQLite supports partial indexes and this one is selective: in a normal wallet most notes are spent.' This is accurate, but a contributor unfamiliar with SQLite partial indexes may not understand why indexing the spent column with a WHERE spent=0 is useful — the indexed column only ever has value 0 in this index, so the B-tree lookup is not for searching, it is purely as a filter/skip mechanism. The comment would be clearer if it said: 'Partial index: instead of scanning all notes, SQLite uses this index to enumerate only unspent notes. The indexed column value is always 0 (the condition), so it functions as a rowid list, not a B-tree search key.'
close_reason: Opinion: current comment is accurate ('Partial index on spent=0 only — selective because most notes are spent'). Elaborating further is bikeshedding.
